seoTitle: Flynn vs Freds Pass — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Flynn and Freds Pass. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Flynn vs Freds Pass: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Flynn and Freds Pass.
heading: Flynn vs Freds Pass — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Flynn, Northern Territory and Freds Pass, Northern Territory. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Flynn has a lower overall climate risk score (32/100) compared to Freds Pass (40/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Flynn or Freds Pass safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Flynn has a flood risk score of 56/100, while Freds Pass scores 60/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Flynn scores 17/100 for bushfire risk. Freds Pass scores 47/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Flynn or Freds Pass?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Flynn has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.43% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
